blocks.text: This species, endemic to Tak province in Thailand, is only known from Mae Tuen Wildlife Sanctuary. Based on georeferenced herbarium specimens, the area of occupancy is estimated as 4 km2, falling within the threshold values for the Critically Endangered category under criterion B2. The major threat to the species is urbanization across its range, which is inferred to be contributing to a continuing decline in habitat area, extent, and quality. The species is considered to be restricted to one location. There is limited population information available, The only known subpopulation comprises eight mature individuals; the population trend is unknown. It is not clear whether the population is severely fragmented or subject to extreme fluctuations. Taking a precautionary approach, the species is assessed as Critically Endangered B2ab(iii); D owing to its restricted AOO, limited number of locations, continuing habitat decline, and highly restricted population size. As the species is poorly studied, and suspected to be under-collected, further research into its distribution and population status will be necessary to validate this assessment of extinction risk.
blocks.text_rich: This species, endemic to Tak province in Thailand, is only known from Mae Tuen Wildlife Sanctuary. Based on georeferenced herbarium specimens, the area of occupancy is estimated as 4 km<sup>2</sup>, falling within the threshold values for the Critically Endangered category under criterion B2. The major threat to the species is urbanization across its range, which is inferred to be contributing to a continuing decline in habitat area, extent, and quality. The species is considered to be restricted to one location. There is limited population information available, The only known subpopulation comprises eight mature individuals; the population trend is unknown. It is not clear whether the population is severely fragmented or subject to extreme fluctuations. Taking a precautionary approach, the species is assessed as Critically Endangered B2ab(iii); D owing to its restricted AOO, limited number of locations, continuing habitat decline, and highly restricted population size. As the species is poorly studied, and suspected to be under-collected, further research into its distribution and population status will be necessary to validate this assessment of extinction risk.

blocks.text: The species is endemic to Thailand. It has only been documented from Mae Tuen Wildlife Sanctuary in Tak province. It occurs in dry deciduous forest between 200-800 m asl. Survey work is recommended to identify whether any further subpopulations occur in Thailand.
blocks.text_rich: The species is endemic to Thailand. It has only been documented from Mae Tuen Wildlife Sanctuary in Tak province. It occurs in dry deciduous forest between 200-800 m asl. Survey work is recommended to identify whether any further subpopulations occur in Thailand.

blocks.text: This species is known from only one subpopulation comprising eight mature individual trees in Mae Tuen Wildlife Sanctuary. The current population trend is unknown. Further research into population status and trends is required to confirmed whether a decline is apparent and ongoing.

rows: Continuing decline in area, extent and/or quality of habitat?

The major threat to the species is urbanisation, which is inferred to be contributing to a continuing decline in quality and habitat area. From 2002 to 2021, Tak lost 5.48kha of humid primary forest, making up 7.4% of its total tree cover loss in the same time period. The total area of humid primary forest in Tak decreased by 1.4% in this time period (Global Forest Watch 2022).

blocks.text: The major threat to the species is urbanization, which is inferred to be contributing to a continuing decline in quality and habitat area. This species is only known from a small subpopulation in Mae Tuen Wildlife Sanctuary. From 2002 to 2021, Tak province lost 5.48 kha of humid primary forest, making up 7.4% of its total tree cover loss in the same time period. The total area of humid primary forest in Tak decreased by 1.4% in this time period (Global Forest Watch 2022).

From 2002 to 2021, Tak province lost 5.48 kha of humid primary forest, making up 7.4% of its total tree cover loss in the same time period. The total area of humid primary forest in Tak decreased by 1.4% in this time period (Global Forest Watch 2022).

blocks.text: The species has been promoted to be a provincial tree (Tak province) and is well protected in Mae Tuen Wildlife Sanctuary. The species is not known to be represented in ex situ conservation in botanic gardens or gene banks (BGCI 2021, Genesys 2021). The species has been listed as Vulnerable in a book of threatened Thai plants (Voradol et al. 2017).

The species has been listed as Vulnerable in a book of threatened Thai plants (Voradol et al. 2017).
